data.description:
## Summary
Two related gaps in the workflow reference-linter (`workflow/lint_refs.py`), surfaced reviewing the code-review gate (epic b744 / WS3):
1. `check_string` is routed over a step's `with:` values + the `if:` guard, but NOT over a `batch:` step's `criteria[].when` expressions (lint_refs.py ~378-407). So a typo'd `${{ steps.X.outputs.include_typo }}` in a batch criterion's `when:` is NEVER name-checked — it only surfaces at runtime.
2. `_output_fields_map` (lint_refs.py ~547) reads only `schema.properties.keys()`, ignoring `patternProperties`. So an output whose dynamic keys live in `patternProperties` (e.g. `overlay_union_output`'s `include_<overlay>` keys, or `plan_review_assemble_criteria_output`'s `include_<ID>`) is invisible to the reference check.

## Impact
The code-review gate (gates/code-review.yaml) leans on 22 `${{ steps.triggers/union.outputs.include_<overlay> }}` refs in batch `when:` clauses; none are linted. A typo would pass `rebar workflow validate` and only fail/misbehave at runtime (silently skipping an overlay). Pre-existing (plan-review's include_<ID> refs have the same blind spot); not introduced by b744.

## Reproduction Steps
1. In a v3 workflow, add a batch criterion `{prompt: X, when: '${{ steps.s.outputs.nonexistent_key }}'}`.
2. `rebar workflow validate <file>` → passes (no 'output not produced' finding), despite the bogus ref.
Expected: the linter flags the unknown output ref (as it does for `with:`/`if:`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `lint_refs` walks `batch.criteria[].when` expressions through `check_string` (same as `if:`/`with:`).
- [ ] `_output_fields_map` consults `patternProperties` (a ref matching a pattern is considered produced), so dynamic-keyed outputs aren't falsely flagged AND a non-matching typo IS flagged.
- [ ] A test: a typo'd batch `when:` ref fails `workflow validate`; a valid `include_<overlay>` ref passes.

## Notes
Discovered reviewing WS3 (8855) of the code-review epic (b744). Not fixed there (linter is shared infra; out of scope for the gate). Workaround: the code-review gate's offline test exercises the real `when:` resolution, catching typos at test time.
data.title: Workflow linter doesn't name-check batch.criteria[].when refs + is patternProperties-blind
